business_model_summary.draft: Uber is a global mobility, delivery, and freight platform that connects consumers, drivers, couriers, merchants, shippers, and carriers through marketplace networks.
business_model_summary.excerpt_or_summary: The business description identifies Uber as a platform operating Mobility, Delivery, and Freight businesses with network participants on both sides of each marketplace.

revenue_driver_summary.draft: Revenue is driven by gross bookings, trips, take rate, advertising and subscription attach, delivery frequency, and freight volume; the key underwriting question is whether scale benefits can keep expanding operating profit.
revenue_driver_summary.source_label: Uber FY2025 Form 10-K revenue and MD&A discussion
revenue_driver_summary.excerpt_or_summary: The filing links revenue and profitability to marketplace activity, take rates, incentives, category mix, and operating leverage across Mobility, Delivery, and Freight.

customer_end_market_summary.draft: End markets include urban mobility riders, restaurants and merchants, consumers ordering local commerce, advertisers, shippers, carriers, drivers, and couriers across many geographies.
customer_end_market_summary.source_label: Uber FY2025 Form 10-K segment and marketplace discussion
customer_end_market_summary.excerpt_or_summary: The filing describes multi-sided marketplaces that serve riders, eaters, merchants, advertisers, shippers, carriers, drivers, and couriers.

business_understanding_risk.draft: The early gate should focus on whether a Senior is comfortable underwriting regulatory, labor-classification, insurance, incentive, and competitive risks while treating missing inventory as expected for a service/platform issuer.
business_understanding_risk.excerpt_or_summary: Risk factors emphasize legal and regulatory constraints, worker classification, insurance cost, competitive intensity, marketplace liquidity, and international operating risk.
